result_note: Mouse is a project-policy short-lived control. Reviewed UniProtKB Swiss-Prot P23804 records Mus musculus MDM2, taxid 10090, a complete 489-aa canonical sequence, and protein-level evidence. Accepted for Gate 7 technical planning only. Aggregate Gate 7 remains closed because TP53 is deferred pending source. No sequence, embedding, structure, or biological claim is produced.

evidence_source_database: NCBI Gene and RefSeq with UniProtKB TrEMBL cross-references
result_note: Rat accession ambiguity is resolved as a terminal source blocker for the current evidence set. NP_001426446.1 remains the validated RefSeq evidence anchor but is not accepted as the canonical project accession or isoform. Active TrEMBL A0A0G2JVC1 is 49 aa longer and contains the RefSeq sequence as an exact subsequence, but is not an exact accession-level match. Active TrEMBL D3ZVH5 is a distinct 458-aa sequence and is not an exact match. A6IGT1 is inactive and excluded. The result is deferred_pending_source with blocker no_unambiguous_canonical_rat_mdm2_sequence.

Hamster is accepted for Gate 7 technical planning through one complete 510-aa sequence group. UniProtKB TrEMBL A0ABM2YB85 and model RefSeq XP_040610761.1 have identical sequence SHA-256 77125856d7835e2b31886052c94d818752c49b9eb7bb86dea1f8d5f313159fe5 and both resolve to GeneID 101833011 Mdm2. Reviewed Swiss-Prot Q60524 and GenBank AAC52425.1 remain a distinct 466-aa legacy fragment group. No canonical biological isoform claim, contrast, embedding, structure, or biological claim is introduced.
